evidence_excerpt: Patient #2 developed granulomatous skin lesions at 6 months, had recurrent diarrhea, and by age 2 had apparent growth retardation; HSCT is planned for both siblings.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: For patient #2: "recurrent diarrhea episodes, and granulomatous skin lesions developed when she was six months old... She is currently two years old and has apparent growth retardation."

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Patient #1: "During followup the patient required additional hospitalizations for pneumonia again."
Patient #1: "inhaled budesonide and salbutamol were given for recurrent wheezing and bronchiolitis."
Patient #1: "The patient is now four years old without apparent growth retardation, infection and autoimmune manifestations."
Patient #1: "now four years old without apparent growth retardation" suggests improved function/growth status.
Patient #2: "granulomatous skin lesions developed when she was six months old despite regular IVIg therapy."
Patient #2: "weight and height percentiles decreased gradually as she had recurrent diarrhea episodes."
Patient #2: "She is currently two years old and has apparent growth retardation."

notes_on_uncertainty: The record describes two siblings with PRKDC-related combined immunodeficiency and differing trajectories. Long-term outcome and quality-of-life categories were assigned to reflect the worst sustained course (patient #2) after diagnosis. Timing of some events (patient #1 readmissions and respiratory symptoms) is not precisely stated; thus, time periods are marked as unknown where needed. HSCT is planned but not yet performed, so it is not listed as a re-intervention.
